chemistry.reaction_name: Photochemical cascade process via flow-based interception of isomerising alkenes
chemistry.mechanism_type: Radical cascade involving alkene isomerization, alkyne cyclization, and electrocyclic reactions
chemistry.product: Polycyclic structures with pentacyclic ring systems (7a-j) or cycloheptatriene intermediates (13)
chemistry.bond_formed: Multiple C-C bonds forming fused ring systems
chemistry.scope_summary: Tolerates various substituents on both aryl rings and alkyne moiety. Electron-donating and electron-withdrawing groups on the aryl rings are compatible. Alkyl and phenyl substituents on the alkyne work well, but cyclopropyl substituent fails. Yields range from 50-81% for final products.

optimization_table.key_findings: 80 mM concentration, 70 W lamp power, 7 min residence time gave 81% yield

key_intermediates.description: diradical intermediate from aldol condensation and Sonogashira reaction

key_observations_supporting_mechanism: Energy profile shows thermodynamically favorable overall reaction with final product at 7.2 kcal/mol
Highest energy barrier is TS6_prod at 69.6 kcal/mol
Multiple spin state crossings indicated by (S) and (T) designations
Photochemical initiation indicated by hν step from EC to int2
figures.caption: Fig. 6 Free-energy profile for the studied reaction of substrate  4a ; S and T refer to singlet and triplet states, respectively. | Fig. 7 Correlation between energy barrier and product formation

key_observations_supporting_mechanism: Substituent effects on activation barriers: R=OMe (52.0 kcal/mol) < R=Me (59.2 kcal/mol) < R=F (60.5 kcal/mol) < R=H (81.8 kcal/mol) < R=CF3 (85.5 kcal/mol) < R=CN (87.6 kcal/mol)
Product yields for compound 7: R=H (81%), R=Me (76%), R=F (52%)
Product yields for compound 13: R=OMe (58%), R=CF3 (22%), R=CN (35%)
figures.caption: Fig. 6 Free-energy profile for the studied reaction of substrate  4a ; S and T refer to singlet and triplet states, respectively. | Fig. 7 Correlation between energy barrier and product formation

translation_logic.batch_limitation: No batch baseline reported in this paper
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Flow enabled the interception of fleeting diradical intermediates and allowed for high-power LED irradiation in short residence times (7 min)
translation_logic.what_changed: New photochemical cascade reaction developed using flow-based strategy
translation_logic.safety_improvement: High-power UV-LED irradiation safely contained in flow reactor

figure_descriptions: Reactor scheme showing high-power UV-LED with coiled reactor geometry
Mechanistic schemes showing photochemical cascade pathway
Energy profile and correlation plots for substituent effects
Scope table showing substrate tolerance and yield ranges

conflicts: Text mentions 375 nm absorption max but light source operates at 365 nm
notes: This is a flow-only paper without batch comparison. Focus is on discovery of new photochemical cascade reactions enabled by flow chemistry. High confidence in optimization parameters from table, moderate confidence in mechanistic details.
